- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. I found the property "U12 has genre" (http://data.doremus.org/ontology#U12_has_genre) which links works to their genre. Now I can construct a query to find recordings of opera works with duration greater than 1 hour.

The approach will be:

tool_calls.name: execute

args.sparql:
SELECT ?recording WHERE { 
  ?work efrbroo:P10217_has_genre ?genre . 
  ?genre rdfs:label 'opera' . 
  ?recording efrbroo:R9i_realises ?work . 
  ?recording efrbroo:U53_has_duration ?duration . 
  FILTER(?duration > 3600) 
}
